<!-- PROSE — edit freely in Obsidian -->

An account of the free school Tolstoy established for peasant children on his Yasnaya Polyana estate in 1859, published in the pedagogical journal of the same name. Tolstoy rejected rote learning and corporal punishment, proposing instead that children learn best through freedom, curiosity, and the teacher's willingness to follow the child's lead. The article documents specific lessons, the children's responses, and Tolstoy's evolving theories of democratic education — ideas that would later influence progressive educators across Europe.
